checks.finding: No issues found. T-001 through T-003 are medium behavioral/specification changes, T-004 and T-005 are high because they affect filesystem trust and approval/review integrity, and T-006 is low metadata-only work.
T-004 and T-005 Done When sections each require pre-change failing tests, stored Red→Green logs, and a named independent reviewer PASS record; no critical-risk task exists.

tasks.md T-005 spans more than three distinct implementation areas: review manifests, deterministic prechecks, reviewer prompts/contracts, workflow-state validation, Bash/PowerShell parity fixtures, and approval-boundary regression. It also combines REQ-016 review-input integrity with REQ-017 approval eligibility. This is unlikely to remain one focused implementation session and risks incomplete verification; split the review-input work from approval-boundary/workflow-state work with an explicit dependency.

acceptance-tests.md AC-001 through AC-005, assigned to functional task T-001, specify only successful required-content assertions. Neither those criteria nor T-001's associated test scope defines a persistent malformed-template or missing-required-section error-path scenario; the initial pre-implementation red run is workflow evidence, not an acceptance error path. A template defect could therefore pass without negative validation, which is a blocking coverage gap.
SKIP: no task is scoped as a dedicated test task; tests are verification work embedded in functional tasks.
T-004 and T-005 each include an explicit Rollback field and an isolated rollback fixture in Done When.
No issues found. The six tasks claim distinct primary implementation surfaces, and shared specification inputs are read-only Must Read dependencies.
No issues found. T-002 consumes the layer artifacts from T-001; T-003 and T-005 consume the layer/index outputs; T-006 is correctly sequenced after all functional changes; T-004 is independent.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.
